channel.title: Comments for page "Dread & Circuses Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Dread & Circuses Hub"

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Image of a star is by Ssolbergj, under CC BY 3.0.

Last image could not be determined to be CC compliant and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quick question, why is the tale "The Great Shucks of the Unnamed Lords" linked here ?
It doesn't have anything to do with the circus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I loved this series. I was sad to see the end of Act III and then went on to read all peripheral articles. Thank you for creating this hub, and I hope to see it expand further.

item.title: SCPs?
item.content:encoded:
I, a membre of the DE-Branch, created a SCP and want to ask how to add it to this canon?

If possible, i will put it on the "Translation Request" page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd totally go to this circus, maybe, probably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just have to say everytime I get caught up on this hub, I get the theme music from Beetlejuice stuck in my head. Not a bad thing, mind you. X3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new banner is by Zhange. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to have contributed to this hub, and glad to plan on contributing in the future~! This whole thing, while maybe a bit too silly at times (I feel), is a blast to read and has given me a couple real, actual laughs. The characters are great and begging to be written for, the world you paint is fun and bubbly while still being utterly dark and disturbing, it's just… except for a couple spots, it really feels nigh perfect. Good job, and I hope to see more.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: The timer's still counting down. Does nothing happen when it hits zero? Does it stop at exactly one second for dramatic effect?

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: The unsourced image has now been removed.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how it's exactly redundant, considering that it's a hub for people to read all the content of their tale series. This is a tale series, and the GoI page is for general navigation of Herman Fuller and all of its tales and skips, along with world building pieces as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you're still missing the point.

For the page itself, you're complaining about a standard practice on this site. To add more examples, Conwell has written several story arcs connected to Anderson Robotics, and each has its own hub page. The Anderson Robotics Hub is an entirely different animal. This is not an unusual thing in any way.

Your other comments are, well, rather odd at best and mildly insulting to Dr.Chandra at worst. It's like you're questioning an author for writing a new book in an ongoing series they've been co-authoring, and then asking Guillermo del Toro if he's aware of the Hellboy comics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel its a bit redundant and ads little to the disquieting corner of the SCP universe…. But its just my opinion. I'm not trying to make any big waves or anything. Also I see more has been added since I last gave my opinion. So theres that too

For anyone else reading ima repeat myself,

Not trying to make waves. My opinion is just that , an opinion. And Also since I first left my opinion I noticed much more was added. When I first gave an opinion it was much more sparsely populated. Now their is much more. Maybe I am missing a point. But that's OK. Don't let that take away from your enjoyments of the disquieting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a tad confused. You've upvoted and positively commented on tales in this series before, but you don't like the hub? I think Mr.Wrong is right, and that you don't understand the different types of hubs.

Yes, I'm aware that there is a hub for Herman Fuller (and found it hard to believe that someone would think otherwise), but that's a hub for the entire GoI. This is for my tale series, and I am by no means the only author to create a hub for a tale series.

For instance, Wills and Ways has its own hub, and this is in no way redundant with the MC&D GoI hub. I created this hub because I realized I was six tales in and still just calling it 'my Fuller series'. Everything on this hub is by me and shares a continuity, making it a distinct subset of The Circus.

I fully acknowledge I am not the first one to suggest that Herman is gone. Peppersghost did that, and as I said in the about section, I am just building on what PeppersGhost started. That's kind of what this whole community is about; sharing ideas and expanding on them.

I agree with Mr.Wrong when he says you should do a little more investigating before making assumptions about authors. If you click the history button at the bottom of any page you can see who created it and who's edited it. If you click any site members name and go to their profile page, you can see pretty much everything they've ever done on the site. I assure you, I have read every Herman Fuller skip and tale, in most cases multiple times, and I was honestly a little baffled that anyone would think I was unfamiliar with the Circus.

As for deletions, articles are deleted if they fall below -9 votes for a period of at least 24 hours, or if they violate the site's terms and services. If an article does not meet at least one of these conditions, I think it's a little rude to suggest it be deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel this is redundant and should be deleted since it already exists.

I think you lack knowledge of what's going on here. There is no rule that there must only be one storyline for every plot thread. There can be multiple stories that account for Fuller's departure from the circus, and they can contradict for all one cares. So long the audience likes it and the rating is favourable, they have a right to exist.

Have you read all the Circus tales?

Based on the author's notes for the various Tales in the series and my interaction with him during critique, I can assure you that DrChandra has knowledge of articles featuring Herman Fuller.

Perhaps you would like to do your background checks before you make such insinuations?

Cause their is already a circus hub.

That hub is the GoI hub, which is effectively just a place for all Herman Fuller articles to be linked from. This hub is specifically for a Tale Series, featuring a specific storyline. They can co-exist together perfectly fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Their has been a few circus tales now with insinuation that Fuller has lost control and is stuck in a fun lovers graveyard somewhere between spaces. Have you read all the Circus tales? Cause their is already a circus hub. I feel this is redundant and should be deleted since it already exists.

http://www.scp-wiki.net/herman-fuller-hub

Just in case you didn't know where to find it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I would recommend you just repost it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I definitely should have finished this in my sandbox and just copied and pasted. Put a blank page up for a few minutes and you get two downvotes. Yeesh. Lesson learned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps starting from a blank page was not the best beginning step. Now the page has content and is no longer blank.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing is complete.

Edit: The image for the Mewlers is by SunnyClockwork. https://78.media.tumblr.com/5a010e8d5ceefa4f541a6ca6af8bc30f/tumblr_pe0uxySRXN1smuenvo1_640.jpg

Edit 2: Source image for the star is from here: http://www.freestockphotos.biz/stockphoto/11013

And Sunny made the tent for me, https://sta.sh/21owdkwkfyiy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pray tell, what are you testing…?
